DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED DOCUMENTS IN APRIL 2004 DOES NOT RELIEVE DEFENDANT OF THE OBLIGATION TO RESPOND TO THE DEMAND AT ISSUE. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SERVE COMPLETE AMENDED VERIFIED RESPONSES AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHIN 21 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ALL OBJECTIONS, OTHER THAN THOSE BASED ON EITHER THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT AND ATTORNEY WORK-PRODUCT PRIVILEGES ARE STRICKEN. IF ANY DOCUMENTS ARE WITHHELD BASED ON SAID PRIVILEGES, DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE COURT MAKES NO FINDINGS AS TO THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED TO DATE. FAILURE TO RESPOND IN A TIMELY FASHION WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $536.30 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 21 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions With Proof Of Service; Memo Of P&A Filed In Support; Declaration Of Anne C. O'Donnell Filed In Support
Off Calendar Sep-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne C. O'donnell for Plaintiff and moving party: Yugi Golf Management

NO OTHER APPEARANCE STATED. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 2023.030 is granted. Defendant is precluded from introducing, either directly or through the use of an expert witness,
any evidence of income or expenses from the restaurant operations for the period of April 15, 2004 to the present other than what had been produced as of August 31, 2005. The Court finds that defendant disobeyed the April 11, 2005 order by serving the amended verified response on August 31, 2005 and by failing to pay the monetary sanctions. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
